title: NQ Upper Liquidity Harvest Then Lower Relocation

summary.thesis: After upper liquidity is fully harvested, the next trade often depends on post-harvest behavior: balance, pullback, or a larger reversal into lower liquidity.
summary.operator_translation: Do not keep chasing the original direction after the objective is already completed. Reassess whether the market is pausing, fading, or fully reversing.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card to warn against late continuation entries after harvest and to highlight lower-liquidity relocation risk.

when_relevant: A visible upper liquidity pocket has already been tagged or consumed.
Immediate continuation starts to lose efficiency after the harvest.
Price begins to shift into balance, pullback, or deeper reversal.
required_evidence: post-harvest balance width or pullback distance is visible
lower liquidity becomes the next meaningful search direction
confirmation_signals: Continuation stalls shortly after the harvest.
Delta and price extension no longer support the original push.
Price starts accepting below the harvested area and searching lower.
invalidation_signals: Post-harvest continuation remains strong and keeps discovering higher prices.
The harvested area is reclaimed and defended again immediately.
There is no meaningful lower relocation despite the harvest.
no_trade_conditions: Do not chase long after the upper objective is already completed unless continuation is still efficient.
Do not assume reversal just because the target was tagged; wait for post-harvest weakness or acceptance lower.
Do not fade inside the immediate harvest area without evidence that the move is actually losing efficiency.

entry_archetypes.name: failed continuation fade
entry_archetypes.description: Fade the exhausted continuation only after post-harvest acceptance lower starts to appear.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Skip if continuation remains impulsive and can still discover higher prices.

entry_archetypes.name: lower liquidity relocation
entry_archetypes.description: Join the move toward lower liquidity after the harvest is complete and local support starts failing.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Skip if the market is only balancing narrowly and not yet choosing a direction.

management_notes: Treat the harvest as one event and the post-harvest response as a separate event family.
Balance after harvest is not automatically reversal; wait for actual relocation behavior.
If lower liquidity is reached, reassess again instead of extrapolating endlessly.
review_questions: Did the operator chase after the target was already completed?
Was there real post-harvest weakness before fading the move?
Did the market actually start searching lower, or only rotate briefly?
Was the chosen trade aligned with post-harvest context rather than pre-harvest logic?
